blueprints.title: Commit code and push to remote repository
blueprints.goal: Prepare a safe commit from local changes and push it to the tracked remote branch.

preconditions.description: The repository has local changes that are intended to be committed.
The target branch and remote are known or can be verified before pushing.

steps.title: Inspect local changes
steps.instruction: Read git status, review the staged and unstaged diff, and inspect recent commit messages before deciding what should be included in the commit.

steps.title: Stage intended files and validate scope
steps.instruction: Stage only the intended files, exclude secrets or unrelated changes, and confirm the final staged diff matches the requested scope.

steps.title: Create commit
steps.instruction: Create a new commit with a concise message that explains why the change exists, then verify the working tree state after the commit.

steps.title: Verify remote push target
steps.instruction: Check the current branch tracking state and confirm which remote branch will receive the push before sending any changes.

steps.title: Push changes
steps.instruction: Push the committed branch to the remote without using force, then report the resulting branch or remote state clearly.

steps.title: Report result
steps.instruction: Summarize what was committed, where it was pushed, and mention any follow-up actions or blockers that remain.

branches.condition: If there are no intended changes to commit, stop and report that no commit should be created.
If the push target is unclear, shared, or potentially risky, stop and confirm the exact branch or remote before pushing.

expectedEvidence.description: Git status and diff were inspected before committing.
The final staged scope was verified before commit.
The target remote branch was verified before push.
A final summary described the commit and push result.

failureModes.title: Commiting unrelated or sensitive files
failureModes.mitigation: Stage files explicitly and inspect the staged diff before creating the commit.

failureModes.title: Pushing to the wrong remote or branch
failureModes.mitigation: Inspect tracking information and name the exact push target before pushing.

failureModes.title: Using destructive git operations as a shortcut
failureModes.mitigation: Prefer a normal push and stop for confirmation before any destructive git action.
